channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans. The motion for emergency relief was filed late Friday in federal court in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia Republicans don’t rule out Senate bids as popular GOP governor remains undecided
item.description: Georgia House Reps. Rich McCormick and Mike Collins left the door open to potential Senate bids in 2026 if Gov. Kemp doesn't run himself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is forcing a generational shift in GOP foreign policy. Here's how Republicans are responding
item.description: Republicans in Congress have long been intent on countering America’s rivals and spreading U.S. influence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gov. DeSantis makes push to repeal Florida's red flag laws
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis signaled that he wants to repeal the state's red flag law, which was approved after the Feb. 2018 mass shooting in Parkland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump withdraws slew of Biden-era lawsuits tied to abortion, racial discrimination, financial regs and more
item.description: President Donald Trump has utilized his new powers to end a slew of lawsuits initiated under former President Joe Biden, including challenges to state abortion bans, complaints of racism at police and fire departments and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: House Republicans have unveiled a 99-page spending bill that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US economic worries mount as Trump implements tariffs, cuts workforce and freezes spending
item.description: President Donald Trump's flurry of tariffs, government layoffs and spending freezes may be doing more to harm the U.S. economy than to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Homeland Security ends TSA collective bargaining agreement, in effort to dismantle union protections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major effort to dismantle union protections under the Trump administratio
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A single day of Trump and Musk's cost-cutting campaign remakes huge sections of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A series of decisions revealed Friday provided a glimpse of the turmoil engulfing federal agencies since President Donald Trump and Elon Musk launched their campaign of disruption, upending how government functions in ways big and small.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri wins $24B judgment against China in COVID lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge ruled in Missouri's favor in its lawsuit against Beijing, accusing China of hoarding personal protective equipment during the coronavirus pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: We live in time: Daylight Savings and the 'Time Lords' of Congress
item.description: Scuffles over Daylight Saving Time and its legislative ilk have roiled Congress for centuries. Here's how the long, tumultuous battle over control of the clock has unfolded so far.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Democrats ask Rubio for answers on now-canceled $400 million Tesla plan
item.description: A letter from two House Democrats presses Rubio for details about who approved an effort to try to use hundreds of millions of dollars of taxpayer money on armored electric vehicles from Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump created a task force Friday to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe’s premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when Trump’s on-again-off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and women and minorities are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to purge diversity, equity and inclusion content.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Housing policies cited as major reason families forced to give up pets
item.description: Pet rescues and animal shelters hear it over and over: a family has to give up their dog because they had to move and the new place has a rule.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration cancels $400M in grants and contracts with Columbia University
item.description: Multiple federal agencies announced Friday that they're canceling grants and contracts because of what the government describes as the Ivy League school’s lackluster efforts to squelch antisemitism on campus. The U of M is one of five colleges under new federal antisemitism investigations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is set to speak to crypto founders and investors at the White House on Friday
item.description: President Trump signed an order late Thursday creating a cryptocurrency strategic reserve using bitcoin seized through court proceedings, directing officials to find “budget-neutral” ways to grow it.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa Bay Ukrainian refugees worried temporary legal status could be revoked
item.description: It has been three years since Russia invaded Ukraine sparking the latest deadly conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dems should recognize issue of border security, de Blasio says
item.description: “Democrats need to say, ‘We believe in order, we believe in security, we need a border that makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Trump illegally removed labor board member
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday ruled that President Trump unlawfully removed a Democratic member of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB), suggesting in her ruling that the president sought to test the limits of his power in doing so. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican medical manufacturers warn tariffs will increase US health care costs
item.description: "We are worried about patients in hospitals because they are the ones who will be affected the most," said Alfredo Dueñez, president of Baja California's Medical Device Cluster.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
